name: Poori (Deep-fried, Puffy Bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the 1 cup of sifted whole-wheat flour, 1 cup of all-purpose flour, and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a large bowl.
2. Drizzle 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil over the dry ingredients. Rub the oil with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs.
3. Gradually add water, stirring to form a stiff dough.
4. Turn the dough onto a clean work surface and knead for 10-12 minutes or until smooth.
5. Shape the dough into a ball and lightly coat it with oil. Place in a plastic bag and let rest for 30 minutes.
6. Divide the dough into 12 equal portions. Keep 11 portions covered.
7. Flatten one portion of dough and roll out to a round about 5-5 1/2 inches in diameter. If possible, keep all pooris in a single layer, covered with plastic wrap.
8. Heat at least 1 inch of oil in a small, deep frying pan over medium flame until very hot.
9. Carefully lift one pauri and place it gently into the hot oil using the back of a slotted spoon. It may sink initially but will sizzle and puff up within seconds.
10. Using the slotted spoon, flip the pauri and cook for about 10 seconds on the second side.
11. Remove with the slotted spoon and place on a plate lined with paper towels.
12. Repeat steps 8-11 until all pooris are cooked. Serve immediately.
